last_analyst_note:
Mode 1 (Section Expansion — EXP-277 close-out of remaining EXP-256 c1/c2 holes) @ 2026-04-29T08:17:50.000Z.

DISPATCHER TRACE (clean clone refreshed at PRELUDE Step 0a):
  Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK.
  Mode 1 — Expansion queue: 3 actionable pending (EXP-277, EXP-278, EXP-280). FIRED on EXP-277 per prior analyst next-run guidance; EXP-280 subsumed (same source artifact, same holes, different reviewer queue runs); EXP-278 deferred to next run.
  Mode 1b — Predictions: ALL DONE.
  Mode 2 — Human notes: NO NOTES.
  Mode 2b — Attention inbox (FUSE): NO ATTENTION ITEMS.
  Mode 3 — Defenses: NO PENDING.
  Mode 4 — Globe fingerprint: ALL DONE.
  Assigned issues: 7 (ISS-1690/1691 → EXP-278 deferred; ISS-1694/1695/1696 → EXP-277 [now ready-for-integration]; ISS-1717/1718 → EXP-280 subsumed_by EXP-277 [resolution proposals written]).

EXP-277 FINDING: Five EXP-256 c1/c2 holes addressed via three exact_string_replace patches. Of the seven c1/c2 holes, five were already RESOLVED-BY-EXP-271 (WCAG contrast at 1.2rem large-text threshold; '0/N met' framing replaced 0/69 conflation; visual order restored thesis→bars→module via EXP-261 v2 + EXP-271 position assertions; sub-meter→sub-0.01% factual fix; ds- prefix on all classes). Two genuinely-open holes patched here:

  P1 (generate-html.js line 1754): 'the August 12, 2026 annular solar eclipse' → 'the August 12, 2026 total solar eclipse'. NASA Besselian magnitude 1.0386 — already correctly stated at line 2949 (Section 2.7) as 'total, not annular'. Annular eclipse of 2026 was February 17 (Antarctic). Module's fm-jump anchor lands readers directly on the eg-falsifiability eclipse pre-commitment paragraph so the error had elevated traffic and was a defender credibility-attack surface. Closes ISS-1718, ISS-1695 part 2, EXP-256 c2 H3.

  P2 (generate-html.js line 489): verdict-bar aria-label noun 'claims' → 'catalog entries'. Visible bar caption (added by EXP-271) already says '71 catalog entries = 69 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims' — this patch propagates that linguistic disambiguation into screen-reader element-nav. Module aria-labels keep 'claims' (those ARE dome-claimed wins; preserves the linguistic distinction). Closes EXP-256 c2 H1 / ISS-1696 part 1.

  P3 (test.js): five new EXP-277 assertions — (1) 'total solar eclipse' present in eg-falsifiability, (2) 'annular solar eclipse' for August 12 absent, (3) verdict-bar aria-labels match /'of N catalog entries'/, (4) legacy 'claims' noun gone from verdict-bar aria-labels, (5) ds-falsifiability-module class appears exactly once (proposal-spec exactly-once check that EXP-271 didn't include — its assertion uses includes() which is at-least-one). Closes ISS-1696 part 2 / ISS-1717 / EXP-256 c2 H2.

KERNEL OF TRUTH: The curmudgeon's EXP-256 c1/c2 reviews caught real holes; EXP-271 already addressed the structural ones in a coordinated patch. What survived is two genuinely substantive items (the eclipse type factual error and the missing exactly-once test) plus the screen-reader version of the denominator-language collision. EXP-271 deserves credit for closing the structural attack surfaces; EXP-277 is housekeeping that closes the residual ones.

OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (per operator user-preference): six counter-attacks anticipated and addressed in EXP-277 anticipated_objections — (1) curmudgeon recommended fm-caption parenthetical not aria-label edit (rebuttal: visible disambiguation already in place from EXP-271; aria-label gap is the residual; aria-label change is invisible to sighted users); (2) why not change module aria-labels instead (rebuttal: 'claims' is correct for module's denominator of 69 dome-claimed wins; bar denominator is catalog entries); (3) is the annular/total fix worth a full EXP (rebuttal: bundled with tests + aria-label closes all five holes in one decider integration); (4) aria-label change ripples (rebuttal: single-occurrence string replace, visible content unchanged, regression caught at test time); (5) why subsume EXP-280 (rebuttal: same holes, different reviewer queues — coordinated patch is the right shape); (6) if EXP-271 fixed most of c1/c2 just close (rebuttal: two genuinely open holes need code patches; five resolved holes get an audit trail).

NEWSWORTHY: false. Internal-quality follow-up; verdicts unchanged.
